113928.explanation: The CDA Ermelo always focuses on resident participation. Elections are once every four years, but we find it important that residents can express their opinions more often. We stand for a government that fundamentally values residents' participation.

113929.explanation: Thinking along with initiatives from society is a moral duty of local government according to CDA Ermelo. We stimulate neighborhood participation in green management by involving residents in design, maintenance, and use of greenery. Right to challenge is a good example of this.

113935.explanation: Good housing for seasonal workers is essential. Employers have a responsibility here, but an obligation on their own premises is not always desirable. CDA Ermelo opts for tailor-made solutions, with attention to livability, spatial quality, and cooperation.

113939.explanation: A fireworks show can contribute to safety and togetherness. At the same time, it is not a core task of the municipality. CDA Ermelo is open to local initiatives and cooperation but does not readily opt for a municipal obligation.

113940.explanation: Good waste collection is important, but free bulky waste collection at home can lead to extra costs and more waste. CDA Ermelo opts for a fair balance: accessible for residents, with attention to affordability, responsibility, and waste prevention.

113941.explanation: CDA Ermelo is pleased with the many events and activities in our village! Events encourage meeting and liveliness. Noise nuisance is now limited by the events policy. Balance with livability.

113943.explanation: CDA Ermelo supports revising the land-use change policy and especially wants to build concentrated near the village core (West). This way, we can spare and protect the open and beautiful countryside. That countryside belongs to the village character of Ermelo.

113946.explanation: CDA Ermelo wants culture and sport to be inclusive and accessible to everyone. We encourage an active sporting, cultural, and social life so that residents can continue to participate.

113947.explanation: CDA Ermelo considers sports clubs indispensable for a livable Ermelo. The government should have a stimulating and facilitating role. Clubs themselves are responsible for a healthy financial policy.

113950.explanation: CDA Ermelo finds it important that residents feel safe in our village. Camera surveillance can contribute to this. It is important that such decisions are made together with residents and entrepreneurs.

113951.explanation: The safety of people in need comes first. CDA Ermelo believes that a women's shelter is essential to provide protection and support to victims of domestic violence, respecting privacy and safety.

113954.explanation: The home seeker comes first for us! Ermelo also has old recreational parks, where transforming the park can offer a solution for both entrepreneurs and society. This is already being addressed with good examples in Horst.

113955.explanation: The home seeker comes first for us! As CDA Ermelo, we support large-scale development of a new residential neighborhood (approximately 300 - 500 homes) developed adjacent to the village center.

113958.explanation: Everyone participates. CDA Ermelo believes that poverty must be effectively combated. By raising the income limit, more residents who cannot make ends meet can receive timely help. This can reduce financial pressure and improve opportunities to participate in society.

113959.explanation: Everyone participates. CDA Ermelo sees youth work as essential for development, social cohesion, and prevention. Budget cuts undermine these goals. Investing in youth ensures a strong, engaged, and resilient community.

113960.explanation: As CDA Ermelo, we take sustainability seriously, based on our deep awareness of care for the world: the earth is not ours, but borrowed. We promote smart energy hubs and energy cooperatives per street or neighborhood.

113961.explanation: As CDA Ermelo, we want to make Ermelo’s residential areas more family-friendly in terms of traffic. We drive 30 km/h where necessary and 50 where possible. Naturally, the primary responsibility for adhering to speed limits lies with the driver.

113963.explanation: Children must grow up with opportunities. CDA Ermelo believes that quality and effectiveness in youth care are paramount. Cheap solutions are not an end in themselves; young people deserve care that fits their needs and well-being.

113966.explanation: We want to relocate Kerkdennen to a new business park near the A28. Of course, this business park must fit Ermelo’s scale and the green character of the rural area. Therefore, no additional business parks will be added in total.

113967.explanation: Sunday is for relaxation. CDA Ermelo supports Sunday rest. Preserving quiet on Sundays, allowing small entrepreneurs to maintain a healthy and social life, and protecting their competitive position in Ermelo are the main reasons.

113968.explanation: CDA Ermelo believes that residents who contribute to our community should have priority for social housing. This strengthens livability, while still considering urgent housing needs of refugees-but not above those of local residents.

113972.explanation: CDA Ermelo believes that the regional dialect contributes to identity, culture, and connection in the municipality. By encouraging attention at primary schools, the Veluwe language remains alive and strengthens local livability and involvement of both young and old.

113974.explanation: The home seeker comes first for us! CDA Ermelo supports appropriate high-rise buildings where this is possible and fitting. Think of spacious park-like environments with many trees or near access roads. Six floors is the maximum, so no high-rise towers.

113976.explanation: Not every neighborhood or district is the same. That is why we must especially look at what is needed: multifunctional facilities and efficient use of existing buildings can sometimes be better. CDA Ermelo finds it important that every neighborhood has a place to meet others.

113978.explanation: CDA Ermelo believes that accessibility and liveliness of the center are important for residents and entrepreneurs. A fully car-free center does not help our entrepreneurs.

113980.explanation: CDA Ermelo supports a local business park near the A28 as an alternative to Kerkdennen. This means local entrepreneurs take precedence and only afterward is it considered whether entrepreneurs from outside Ermelo get space on the business park.

113981.explanation: CDA Ermelo believes everyone should be able to enjoy the nature of Ermelo. Leashed dogs protect flora, fauna, and other visitors. The responsibility lies with the dog owner to ensure a safe and pleasant environment for everyone.

113928.explanation: Eén-Ermelo strongly commits to transparency, early involvement, and the Right to Challenge. A referendum fits with more decision-making power for residents.

113929.explanation: Eén-Ermelo opts for "volunteers first" and the Right to Challenge. If residents can do it better or cheaper, they should be able to – with clear agreements.

113935.explanation: This prevents pressure on the housing market and aligns with the principle that the user pays and bears responsibility.

113939.explanation: A central fireworks display helps with acceptance of a fireworks ban. If the municipality offers a safe and attractive alternative, residents are more likely to comply with the rules.

113940.explanation: Eén-Ermelo wants to use public funds wisely. Free collection sounds attractive, but it costs a lot. First look at efficiency and fair rates.

113941.explanation: Eén-Ermelo wants clear agreements between organizers and neighbors, so events can continue without nuisance. Stricter regulations are not always better; predictability is.

113943.explanation: Eén-Ermelo protects the open landscape of Telgt and Horst. Housing belongs to the village center and designated expansion sites, not scattered in the countryside.

113946.explanation: Eén-Ermelo invests in culture but does not make firm promises for universal music lessons. There is room for initiatives via cultural funds and schools.

113947.explanation: Eén-Ermelo invests in sports as prevention and as a binding factor in the village. Clubs that meet their agreements can count on support.

113950.explanation: Cameras can help with persistent nuisance, but only where really necessary and temporarily. First listen, then deploy targeted measures.

113951.explanation: Eén-Ermelo strongly focuses on prevention of domestic violence and femicide, quick detection, and good shelter. Having a dedicated facility is possible but is not always the best or fastest solution.

113954.explanation: Eén-Ermelo wants to accelerate the transformation of holiday parks. Where possible and appropriate, this helps solve the housing shortage.

113955.explanation: Ermelo needs space for starters, families, and seniors. Eén-Ermelo chooses expansion in logical locations such as Kerkdennen, Driehoek, and Tonsel.

113958.explanation: The current income limit in Ermelo is already generous and provides sufficient support. Further increases make policy less focused and unnecessarily pressure the budget.

113959.explanation: Youth work is prevention. It prevents problems on the street, supports families, and helps young people get started. Budget cuts cost more later.

113960.explanation: Eén-Ermelo chooses practical sustainability. Charging stations belong to the future, but must be fairly distributed and not unnecessarily stress parking.

113961.explanation: Eén-Ermelo chooses 30 km/h as the standard in residential areas and school zones, but not on all main roads. On some main roads, 50 km/h remains logical for traffic flow and safety.

113963.explanation: Quality first, strict against healthcare fraud, and prevention via schools and sports clubs. Focusing only on price leads to problems.

113966.explanation: It is important that we utilize business parks near the A28 to facilitate the autonomous growth of local businesses. This way we keep space for Ermelo entrepreneurs and strengthen the local economy.

113967.explanation: Eén-Ermelo opts for custom solutions: calm where needed, vibrant where possible. Sunday shopping only where there is support.

113968.explanation: Eén-Ermelo chooses housing priority for Ermelo residents. Local youth, starters, and families should be helped first.

113972.explanation: This fits the identity of Ermelo. Not obligatory, but should be made possible where schools and parents want it.

113974.explanation: Eén-Ermelo chooses for village character, mixed neighborhoods, and human scale. High-rise flats do not fit Ermelo.

113976.explanation: Community centers and meeting places are important for livability. They strengthen social cohesion, support volunteers, and provide each district a place to meet.

113978.explanation: Eén-Ermelo chooses accessibility without hassle. Car-free is too rigid; tailor-made solutions and good traffic flow work better.

113980.explanation: Eén-Ermelo chooses an economy with character, but no closed doors. Local businesses get opportunities, but exclusion does not work.

113981.explanation: The dog is not the problem on the heath. Dogs and their owners can continue to enjoy it together, as long as everyone behaves properly and respects nature and other visitors.

113928.explanation: We prefer to involve residents early in plans. A referendum can be held sometimes, but it is not our main instrument.

113929.explanation: Initiatives from residents are valuable and deserve support if they are feasible and strengthen the community.

113935.explanation: Employers provide housing and bear responsibility for good living conditions.

113939.explanation: We choose safety and livability. A joint fireworks or light show, for example at Horst beach, is a good alternative.

113940.explanation: Possible at certain moments, to support residents who cannot do this themselves.

113941.explanation: We want to limit nuisance and protect livability.

113943.explanation: We encourage change of use and affordable housing if this fits in the rural area.

113946.explanation: Every child deserves equal opportunities to develop.

113947.explanation: Sports clubs are important for meeting and health, but with clear agreements.

113950.explanation: Safety is important, but privacy also matters.

113951.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be safely accommodated.

113954.explanation: Not definitively permitted, because Ermelo is a recreational village.

113955.explanation: Infill and redevelopment take priority. Expansion is possible if it is small-scale and fits well in the landscape.

113958.explanation: This way we help more people who have financial difficulties.

113959.explanation: Youth work is important for prevention and support.

113960.explanation: More charging points make sustainable transport possible for more residents.

113961.explanation: Lower speed leads to more traffic safety and livability.

113963.explanation: Quality of care takes precedence over the lowest price.

113966.explanation: We do not support general expansion of industrial areas. Along the A28 there is an ongoing development for local entrepreneurs.

113967.explanation: No further relaxation of Sunday opening hours; peace and meeting are important.

113968.explanation: Refugees with a status must be housed. That is a legal and human responsibility.

113972.explanation: Local culture is valuable, but schools decide the content themselves.

113974.explanation: Building must fit the village and the surroundings. Sometimes building upwards is necessary.

113976.explanation: Meeting places and community centers are important for livability and community.

113978.explanation: Accessibility and livability must be balanced.

113980.explanation: Ecopark is intended for Ermelo companies that want to relocate and become more sustainable.

113981.explanation: We focus on responsible behavior of dog owners.

113928.explanation: The municipality is open to the ideas, wishes, and needs of residents. When an issue is very important, a referendum can be a good and fair way to make a decision. This way we involve people in local democracy.

113929.explanation: If you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, beautiful things arise. The municipality should not hinder this but rather encourage it.

113935.explanation: Progressief Ermelo believes housing on own property is only permitted if workers' health is guaranteed. We are against glyphosate and advocate for pesticide-free zones around living places. Healthy housing is an employer’s obligation, while preserving our valuable landscape.

113939.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one large fireworks show organized by the municipality. This way, we keep it safe and can all enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

113940.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

113941.explanation: The location 'Het Weitje' for activities is not ideal due to nuisance for residents nearby. But because of the existing hospitality around Het Weitje and its central location, this remains for us a place for events. According to our party, there will be no expansion of activities at Het Weitje.

113943.explanation: Progressief Ermelo wants to facilitate farmers by converting old buildings into homes via the red-for-red scheme. We are neutral: building on own land is allowed, provided homes are clustered to prevent fragmentation of the rural area. Landscape preservation is central.

113946.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

113947.explanation: Progressief Ermelo is in favor. Sports clubs are vital meeting places that contribute to health and a strong social network. If a club is at risk of closing, the whole neighborhood suffers. Therefore, the municipality must step in to preserve this social capital.

113950.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

113951.explanation: Progressief Ermelo is in favor. The municipality must provide good shelter and protection to residents who are unsafe at home. We support the establishment of a shelter and want the municipality to immediately remove perpetrators from the home through a preventive restraining order, even before a conviction.

113954.explanation: Progressief Ermelo supports the transformation of parks within the Vital Holiday Parks project into residential areas. This is crucial for the safety and health of residents and to prevent undermining. This way, we create legal places to stay and a healthy living environment for everyone.

113955.explanation: Progressief Ermelo wants to build for the future without sacrificing our precious countryside. Therefore, we specifically designate one location between Ermelo and Drielanden. This allows us to optimally use existing facilities and protect nature elsewhere from unnecessary expansion beyond current boundaries.

113958.explanation: Financial worries should never be a barrier to participation. By raising the income threshold, we prevent financial stress in more households. Progressief Ermelo chooses a fair poverty policy that increases opportunities and prevents bigger problems in the future.

113959.explanation: Cutting back on youth work is an expensive mistake. Progressief Ermelo opts for prevention through youth coaches to avoid more intensive care. We invest in calm, housing, and financial support so vulnerable young people can get their lives back on track. Prevention is better than cure.

113960.explanation: Good facilities are indispensable to encourage the switch to sustainable transport. Progressief Ermelo focuses on better provisions for electric (shared) cars. More charging points make electric driving accessible and directly contribute to our local climate goals.

113961.explanation: Safety for pedestrians and cyclists comes first. Progressief Ermelo wants to lower the speed limit to 30 km/h where possible, supported by designating roads as bicycle streets. This is how we enforce safety in places where drivers currently drive too fast, while keeping traffic flow in mind.

113963.explanation: Quality and trust must be central, not the lowest price. Progressief Ermelo wants to move away from the current tendering system. We choose care that meets the child’s needs and explore new ways to procure care without excessive market mechanisms.

113966.explanation: Progressief Ermelo rejects large-scale industry. We only want a small-scale, energy-neutral park along the A28 to relocate nuisance-causing businesses from Kerkdennen. This takes heavy traffic out of residential neighborhoods and preserves peace and livability in the village.

113967.explanation: Besides supermarkets, Progressief Ermelo also wants to give other retailers the freedom to open on Sunday afternoons (12:00-18:00). We believe entrepreneurs themselves should be able to choose whether they want to welcome visitors on Sundays, consistent with a contemporary and hospitable Ermelo.

113968.explanation: The housing of permit holders is established by regional agreements and legal tasks. Progressief Ermelo complies with these agreements, but primarily focuses on significantly more social housing for all home seekers, so that waiting times for everyone in Ermelo become shorter.

113972.explanation: Heritage is more than buildings; it also includes our regional language and traditions. Progressief Ermelo wants to keep this alive for future generations. By facilitating attention at schools, we connect our past with the future and increase young people’s involvement with their own region.

113974.explanation: Progressief Ermelo sees opportunities for high-rise buildings, provided the location and integration fit the environment. Examples like Het Kasteel show that this is possible without undermining the village character. This way, we build compactly and preserve space elsewhere in the village for green areas and livability.

113976.explanation: Progressief Ermelo finds an own meeting place in each neighborhood and village crucial to combat loneliness. This does not need to be a large center; a community center, school, or even a picnic bench can serve as a place for contact and connection between residents.

113978.explanation: Progressief Ermelo wants a car-free Raadhuisplein and a low-traffic center. This creates more space for culture, encounters, and a lively atmosphere. By reducing car traffic and not expanding parking spaces, we make Ermelo’s center attractive and livable.

113980.explanation: For Progressief Ermelo, the new park near the A28 is the place to relocate local companies from Kerkdennen. This way, we remove nuisance and heavy traffic from residential neighborhoods. We reserve this scarce space for Ermelo entrepreneurs and reject large-scale external industry.

113981.explanation: Although the heath is a Natura 2000 area and nature restoration is a priority, it is also a scarce off-leash area. Progressief Ermelo opts for zoning and mutual respect instead of a general on-leash obligation. This way, we protect nature and maintain space for recreation.

113928.explanation: The SGP does not want referendums because they do not do justice to the responsibility of the government.

113929.explanation: Local initiatives are encouraged to promote resident participation. To sustain this in the long term, however, it remains a point of attention.

113935.explanation: Entrepreneurs are held responsible for employees they bring here, including aspects such as housing and learning the language.

113939.explanation: We should carefully consider how to keep the situation around New Year's Eve safe. Possibly deploying a drone show.

113940.explanation: Reducing waste and improving waste separation are central. The polluter pays, but waste collection must remain cost-effective and accessible. As a municipality, we look differently at waste because material reuse is possible in many cases.

113941.explanation: More enforcement is needed regarding order and safety at hospitality venues and large events to prevent noise nuisance and unsafe situations before, during, or after events.

113943.explanation: The policy on change of use must be enforced. A second company house at agricultural businesses may be allowed if there is demonstrated necessity or a large livestock population.

113946.explanation: The SGP does not prioritize this.

113947.explanation: The SGP appreciates when municipal sports facilities operate independently.

113950.explanation: CCTV surveillance is used to increase the safety of shopkeepers and the public, especially in the center.

113951.explanation: It is important that women in emergency situations can be offered a safe home. This could also be in a neighboring municipality or arranged regionally.

113954.explanation: Holiday homes and permanent residences next to each other, for example on a holiday park, cause problems between residents and vacationers.

113955.explanation: Focus must be on large-scale housing locations: a target of at least 1,820 homes for the period 2025-2034 should be adopted to ensure sufficient housing in the future.

113958.explanation: The municipality must prevent the poverty trap by contributing to making (more) work financially more attractive than receiving benefits.

113959.explanation: Young people can get into trouble due to conscious or unconscious circumstances. This can be caused by addictions, as well as parental relationship problems or mental health issues. Young people deserve the chance to develop well to prevent potential problems.

113960.explanation: (More) (fast) charging stations will be installed in public spaces to stimulate electric transport.

113961.explanation: 50 km/h as a maximum speed within built-up areas is not entirely ruled out.

113963.explanation: The quality of youth care is important, not just the price.

113966.explanation: Expansion of new work locations through creation of a new industrial park, for example along the A28. There is urgent need for new business sites.

113967.explanation: No economic and commercial activities on Sundays and keeping shops closed on Sundays.

113968.explanation: Status holders are not given priority over existing waiting lists of local residents for a (social) rental home.

113972.explanation: We do not have a fully developed opinion on this. If attention is paid to it, it can be on a voluntary basis.

113974.explanation: The outcome of the social council program from a few years ago indicated that residents do not choose this.

113976.explanation: Social district teams remain low-threshold and accessible for direct support within the neighborhood, with a meeting place in every neighborhood.

113978.explanation: That is bad for entrepreneurs and shopkeepers.

113980.explanation: The realization of new business parks is mainly for local companies.

113981.explanation: Possibly designate part of the heath as an off-leash area.

113928.explanation: Citizen participation is important. But complex issues often cannot be reduced to a simple yes/no choice. Also, a low turnout can result in a small group deciding for everyone.

113929.explanation: The choice here is truly issue-specific. Taking over management of a sports hall involves significant financial risks and responsibilities. You don't want to place this solely on residents.

113935.explanation: Housing of seasonal workers is the responsibility of the employer, which also reduces pressure on the housing market.

113939.explanation: This costs money and comes at the expense of other facilities.

113940.explanation: Free bulky waste collection encourages proper waste disposal and offers convenience to residents, but it also costs a lot of money and often leads to unfair distribution while other alternatives are possible.

113941.explanation: Existing regulations must be better enforced.

113943.explanation: There definitely need to be fewer rules regarding obtaining building permits, but free building is not an automatic right.

113946.explanation: Music lessons do not belong to the basic needs of children.

113947.explanation: Local sports clubs must be preserved, but there is also individual responsibility, and clubs can seek other sources of income.

113950.explanation: Camera surveillance in the center can help improve safety in the city center.

113951.explanation: There are sufficient alternatives for this in the region.

113954.explanation: Living in selected parks helps to combat the housing shortage.

113955.explanation: No large-scale housing construction in the rural areas; there are sufficient opportunities within the village cores that remain untapped.

113958.explanation: The income threshold for poverty is determined nationally. Raising this threshold costs a lot of money and comes at the expense of other shared facilities.

113959.explanation: There must be more personal responsibility. Young people and parents can do more themselves.

113960.explanation: This can be an incentive for sustainability but it also costs normal parking spaces. Not everyone drives electric, and costs for installation and maintenance are high.

113961.explanation: In specific locations within the municipality, this can make the situation safer. In other places, this may not be necessary.

113963.explanation: There must be a proper balance between quality and price.

113966.explanation: In Ermelo, there must be sufficient business parks to meet local demand.

113967.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneur to decide if and when the store is open.

113968.explanation: It is not fair to the housing seekers in our municipality to give refugees priority for social rental housing.

113972.explanation: It is more important to ensure that all children acquire sufficient learning skills to fully participate in society later.

113974.explanation: Before switching to tall residential buildings, all other possibilities must first be utilized. There are still many unused locations where housing is possible.

113976.explanation: Many hamlets in Ermelo have relatively very few residents. Having their own village center would come at the expense of other important facilities.

113978.explanation: A large share of our residents come from the rural areas to the center. The car is an important means of transport for this.

113980.explanation: The new A28 business park is mainly intended for companies from Ermelo.

113981.explanation: A mandatory leash law on the Groevenbeek Heath does not fit with the VVD: personal responsibility, enforcement of existing rules, and preservation of recreational freedom are paramount.

113928.explanation: Council members are the representatives of the people.

113941.explanation: As is the case at football matches.

113943.explanation: Not by themselves, but farmers must be made it easier.

113946.explanation: Music education is provided in primary education.

113947.explanation: Then the membership fee must increase.

113951.explanation: No signals received yet that this is necessary

113954.explanation: We have no problems with that and it provides a solution for the housing shortage for starters and in cases of divorce, etc.

113955.explanation: Well-considered new construction can enrich the rural area

113959.explanation: That can be done more efficiently with, for example, good triage

113960.explanation: That is a problem for the electric car owner themselves. They can install a charging station at home.

113961.explanation: Distances within the built-up area are limited and it makes the village friendlier.

113963.explanation: It can and must be cheaper

113966.explanation: We urgently need the physical space for housing construction

113967.explanation: Limited Sunday opening for every entrepreneur who wants it.

113968.explanation: That seems undesirable to us considering the housing shortage of our residents

113972.explanation: We do not consider it useful to structurally pay attention to a Veluwe regional language, but we welcome it if a song in the regional language is occasionally taught.

113978.explanation: Distances in the center are not too long

113980.explanation: But that seems impossible according to the Province's conditions

113981.explanation: Not yet, but with more biting incidents or accidents involving riders, it will be.

statements.title: Only businesses from Ermelo may be allowed on the new business park by the A28.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents of Ermelo should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Ermelo moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
More camera surveillance should be installed in Ermelo.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
There should be a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence in Ermelo.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Veluwe regional dialect.
More business parks should be created in Ermelo.
All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The centre of Ermelo should become car-free.
High-rise residential buildings may be allowed in Ermelo.
It should be mandatory to keep dogs on a leash on the Groevenbeek Heath.
